[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_update_pr_body.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="ci-update-pr-body")
@click.option("--plan-id", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier (for context)")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.option("--planned-pr", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Planned-PR plan")
@click.pass_context
def ci_update_pr_body(
    ctx: click.Context,
    plan_id: int,
    run_id: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
    planned_pr: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="ci-update-pr-body")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier (for context)")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.option("--planned-pr", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Planned-PR plan")
@click.pass_context
def ci_update_pr_body(
    ctx: click.Context,
    pr_number: int,
    run_id: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
    planned_pr: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_update_pr_body.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="ci-update-pr-body")
@click.option("--plan-id", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier (for context)")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.option("--planned-pr", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Planned-PR plan")
@click.pass_context
def ci_update_pr_body(
    ctx: click.Context,
    plan_id: int,
    run_id: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
    planned_pr: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="ci-update-pr-body")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier (for context)")
@click.option("--run-id", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run ID")
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.option("--planned-pr", is_flag=True, default=False, help="Planned-PR plan")
@click.pass_context
def ci_update_pr_body(
    ctx: click.Context,
    pr_number: int,
    run_id: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
    planned_pr: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py","old_string":"@dataclass(frozen=True)
class HandleNoChangesSuccess:
    """Success result when no-changes is handled."""

    success: bool
    pr_number: int
    plan_id: int","new_string":"@dataclass(frozen=True)
class HandleNoChangesSuccess:
    """Success result when no-changes is handled."""

    success: bool
    pr_number: int"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py","old_string":"def _build_no_changes_title(*, plan_id: int, original_title: str) -> str:
    """Build PR title indicating no changes were produced.

    Args:
        plan_id: Plan identifier
        original_title: Original PR title

    Returns:
        New title with [no-changes] prefix and plan reference
    """
    return f"[no-changes] #{plan_id} Impl Attempt: {original_title}"","new_string":"def _build_no_changes_title(*, pr_number: int, original_title: str) -> str:
    """Build PR title indicating no changes were produced.

    Args:
        pr_number: Plan identifier
        original_title: Original PR title

    Returns:
        New title with [no-changes] prefix and plan reference
    """
    return f"[no-changes] #{pr_number} Impl Attempt: {original_title}""}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py","old_string":"def _build_pr_body(
    *,
    plan_id: int,
    behind_count: int,
    base_branch: str,
    recent_commits: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
) -> str:
    """Build PR body explaining why no changes were made.

    Args:
        plan_id: Plan identifier for linking
        behind_count: How many commits behind base branch
        base_branch: Base branch name
        recent_commits: Recent commits on base branch (newline-separated)
        run_url: Optional workflow run URL

    Returns:
        Formatted PR body markdown
    """","new_string":"def _build_pr_body(
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    behind_count: int,
    base_branch: str,
    recent_commits: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
) -> str:
    """Build PR body explaining why no changes were made.

    Args:
        pr_number: Plan identifier for linking
        behind_count: How many commits behind base branch
        base_branch: Base branch name
        recent_commits: Recent commits on base branch (newline-separated)
        run_url: Optional workflow run URL

    Returns:
        Formatted PR body markdown
    """"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="handle-no-changes")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="PR number to update")
@click.option("--plan-id", type=int, required=True, help="Plan identifier")
@click.option("--behind-count", type=int, required=True, help="How many commits behind base branch")
@click.option("--base-branch", type=str, required=True, help="Base branch name")
@click.option("--original-title", type=str, required=True, help="Original PR title")
@click.option(
    "--recent-commits",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="Recent commits on base branch (newline-separated)",
)
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.pass_context
def handle_no_changes(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    plan_id: int,
    behind_count: int,
    base_branch: str,
    original_title: str,
    recent_commits: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="handle-no-changes")
@click.option("--pr-number", type=int, required=True, help="PR number to update")
@click.option("--behind-count", type=int, required=True, help="How many commits behind base branch")
@click.option("--base-branch", type=str, required=True, help="Base branch name")
@click.option("--original-title", type=str, required=True, help="Original PR title")
@click.option(
    "--recent-commits",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="Recent commits on base branch (newline-separated)",
)
@click.option("--run-url", type=str, default=None, help="Optional workflow run URL")
@click.pass_context
def handle_no_changes(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_number: int,
    behind_count: int,
    base_branch: str,
    original_title: str,
    recent_commits: str | None,
    run_url: str | None,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py","old_string":"    # Build PR title and body
    new_title = _build_no_changes_title(plan_id=plan_id, original_title=original_title)
    pr_body = _build_pr_body(
        plan_id=plan_id,
        behind_count=behind_count,
        base_branch=base_branch,
        recent_commits=recent_commits,
        run_url=run_url,
    )","new_string":"    # Build PR title and body
    new_title = _build_no_changes_title(pr_number=pr_number, original_title=original_title)
    pr_body = _build_pr_body(
        pr_number=pr_number,
        behind_count=behind_count,
        base_branch=base_branch,
        recent_commits=recent_commits,
        run_url=run_url,
    )"}

    try:
        backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            str(plan_id),
            metadata={"lifecycle_stage": "impl"},
        )","new_string":"    # 3. Set lifecycle stage to impl
    try:

            str(pr_number),

    try:
        comment = _build_issue_comment(pr_number=pr_number)

        backend.add_comment(repo_root, str(pr_number), comment)"}

    result = HandleNoChangesSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=pr_number,
        plan_id=plan_id,
    )
    click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))","new_string":"    # Success - output JSON and exit 0
    result = HandleNoChangesSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=pr_number,
    )

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_result.py","old_string":"@click.command(name="track-learn-result")
@click.option(
    "--plan-id",
    required=True,
    type=str,
    help="Plan identifier (e.g., issue number)",
)
@click.option(
    "--status",
    required=True,
    type=click.Choice(["completed_no_plan", "completed_with_plan", "pending_review"]),
    help="Learn workflow result status",
)
@click.option(
    "--learn-plan",
    type=int,
    help="Learn plan number (required if status is completed_with_plan)",
)
@click.option(
    "--plan-pr",
    type=int,
    help="Learn documentation PR number (required if status is pending_review)",
)
@click.pass_context
def track_learn_result(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    plan_id: str,
    status: str,
    learn_plan: int | None,
    plan_pr: int | None,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="track-learn-result")
@click.option(
    "--pr-id",
    required=True,
    type=str,
    help="Plan identifier (e.g., issue number)",
)
@click.option(
    "--status",
    required=True,
    type=click.Choice(["completed_no_plan", "completed_with_plan", "pending_review"]),
    help="Learn workflow result status",
)
@click.option(
    "--learn-plan",
    type=int,
    help="Learn plan number (required if status is completed_with_plan)",
)
@click.option(
    "--plan-pr",
    type=int,
    help="Learn documentation PR number (required if status is pending_review)",
)
@click.pass_context
def track_learn_result(
    ctx: click.Context,
    *,
    pr_id: str,
    status: str,
    learn_plan: int | None,
    plan_pr: int | None,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_result.py","old_string":"    # Update plan-header with learn result via PlanBackend
    try:
        backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            plan_id,
            metadata={
                "learn_status": learn_status,
                "learn_plan_issue": learn_plan,
                "learn_plan_pr": plan_pr,
            },
        )
    except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="no-metadata-block",
            message=(
                f"Plan {plan_id} has no plan-header metadata block — cannot update learn status"
            ),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None
    except RuntimeError as e:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=f"Failed to update learn status on plan {plan_id}: {e}",
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    result = TrackLearnResultSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=plan_id,
        learn_status=status,
        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan,
        learn_plan_pr=plan_pr,
    )

    click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))","new_string":"    # Update plan-header with learn result via PlanBackend
    try:
        backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            pr_id,
            metadata={
                "learn_status": learn_status,
                "learn_plan_issue": learn_plan,
                "learn_plan_pr": plan_pr,
            },
        )
    except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="no-metadata-block",
            message=(
                f"Plan {pr_id} has no plan-header metadata block — cannot update learn status"
            ),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None
    except RuntimeError as e:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=f"Failed to update learn status on plan {pr_id}: {e}",
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    result = TrackLearnResultSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=pr_id,
        learn_status=status,
        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan,
        learn_plan_pr=plan_pr,
    )

    click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/track_learn_result.py","old_string":"    # Update plan-header with learn result via PlanBackend
    try:
        backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            plan_id,
            metadata={
                "learn_status": learn_status,
                "learn_plan_issue": learn_plan,
                "learn_plan_pr": plan_pr,
            },
        )
    except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="no-metadata-block",
            message=(
                f"Plan {plan_id} has no plan-header metadata block — cannot update learn status"
            ),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None
    except RuntimeError as e:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=f"Failed to update learn status on plan {plan_id}: {e}",
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    result = TrackLearnResultSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=plan_id,
        learn_status=status,
        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan,
        learn_plan_pr=plan_pr,
    )

    click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))","new_string":"    # Update plan-header with learn result via PlanBackend
    try:
        backend.update_metadata(
            repo_root,
            pr_id,
            metadata={
                "learn_status": learn_status,
                "learn_plan_issue": learn_plan,
                "learn_plan_pr": plan_pr,
            },
        )
    except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="no-metadata-block",
            message=(
                f"Plan {pr_id} has no plan-header metadata block — cannot update learn status"
            ),
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None
    except RuntimeError as e:
        error = TrackLearnResultError(
            success=False,
            error="github-api-failed",
            message=f"Failed to update learn status on plan {pr_id}: {e}",
        )
        click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(error)), err=True)
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    result = TrackLearnResultSuccess(
        success=True,
        pr_number=pr_id,
        learn_status=status,
        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan,
        learn_plan_pr=plan_pr,
    )

    click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(result), indent=2))"}

... [64 lines omitted] ...

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_session.py","old_string":"    """Preprocess and push a session to the planned-pr-context branch with accumulation.

    Preprocesses the session JSONL to compressed XML, then pushes the XML files
    to the planned-pr-context/{plan_id} branch, accumulating across lifecycle stages.
    Updates the manifest and plan metadata.

    Always exits with code 0 (non-critical operation).
    """
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    git = require_git(ctx)
    time = require_time(ctx)

    # Step 0: Compute provenance stats from raw JSONL (before preprocessing)
    provenance = compute_session_provenance(session_file)

    # Step 1: Preprocess session to XML
    xml_files = _preprocess_session(
        session_file=session_file,
        stage=stage,
        session_id=session_id,
        repo_root=repo_root,
    )

    if xml_files is None:
        click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "preprocessing_failed"}))
        return

    # Compute XML size from generated files
    xml_size_kb = sum(f.stat().st_size for f in xml_files) // 1024

    # Get the current git branch for provenance
    git_branch = git.branch.get_current_branch(repo_root)

    session_branch = f"planned-pr-context/{plan_id}"
    timestamp = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()","new_string":"    """Preprocess and push a session to the planned-pr-context branch with accumulation.

    Preprocesses the session JSONL to compressed XML, then pushes the XML files
    to the planned-pr-context/{pr_number} branch, accumulating across lifecycle stages.
    Updates the manifest and plan metadata.

    Always exits with code 0 (non-critical operation).
    """
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    git = require_git(ctx)
    time = require_time(ctx)

    # Step 0: Compute provenance stats from raw JSONL (before preprocessing)
    provenance = compute_session_provenance(session_file)

    # Step 1: Preprocess session to XML
    xml_files = _preprocess_session(
        session_file=session_file,
        stage=stage,
        session_id=session_id,
        repo_root=repo_root,
    )

    if xml_files is None:
        click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "preprocessing_failed"}))
        return

    # Compute XML size from generated files
    xml_size_kb = sum(f.stat().st_size for f in xml_files) // 1024

    # Get the current git branch for provenance
    git_branch = git.branch.get_current_branch(repo_root)

    session_branch = f"planned-pr-context/{pr_number}"
    timestamp = time.now().replace(tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_session.py","old_string":"    # Step 7: Update plan metadata
    backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
    plan_id_str = str(plan_id)
    metadata: dict[str, object] = {
        "last_session_branch": session_branch,
        "last_session_id": session_id,
        "last_session_at": timestamp,
        "last_session_source": source,
    }

    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id_str)
    if not isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        try:
            backend.update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id_str, metadata)
        except RuntimeError:
            pass  # Non-critical: session branch was still created

    # Step 8: Clean up scratch files
    scratch_dir = repo_root / ".erk" / "scratch" / "push-session"
    if scratch_dir.exists():
        for f in scratch_dir.iterdir():
            if f.is_file():
                f.unlink()

    result_output: dict[str, object] = {
        "uploaded": True,
        "pr_number": plan_id,
        "session_branch": session_branch,
        "session_id": session_id,
        "stage": stage,
        "files": filenames,
    }
    click.echo(json.dumps(result_output))","new_string":"    # Step 7: Update plan metadata
    backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
    pr_number_str = str(pr_number)
    metadata: dict[str, object] = {
        "last_session_branch": session_branch,
        "last_session_id": session_id,
        "last_session_at": timestamp,
        "last_session_source": source,
    }

    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, pr_number_str)
    if not isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        try:
            backend.update_metadata(repo_root, pr_number_str, metadata)
        except RuntimeError:
            pass  # Non-critical: session branch was still created

    # Step 8: Clean up scratch files
    scratch_dir = repo_root / ".erk" / "scratch" / "push-session"
    if scratch_dir.exists():
        for f in scratch_dir.iterdir():
            if f.is_file():
                f.unlink()

    result_output: dict[str, object] = {
        "uploaded": True,
        "pr_number": pr_number,
        "session_branch": session_branch,
        "session_id": session_id,
        "stage": stage,
        "files": filenames,
    }
    click.echo(json.dumps(result_output))"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_session.py","old_string":"    # Step 7: Update plan metadata
    backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
    plan_id_str = str(plan_id)
    metadata: dict[str, object] = {
        "last_session_branch": session_branch,
        "last_session_id": session_id,
        "last_session_at": timestamp,
        "last_session_source": source,
    }

    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id_str)
    if not isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        try:
            backend.update_metadata(repo_root, plan_id_str, metadata)
        except RuntimeError:
            pass  # Non-critical: session branch was still created

    # Step 8: Clean up scratch files
    scratch_dir = repo_root / ".erk" / "scratch" / "push-session"
    if scratch_dir.exists():
        for f in scratch_dir.iterdir():
            if f.is_file():
                f.unlink()

    result_output: dict[str, object] = {
        "uploaded": True,
        "pr_number": plan_id,
        "session_branch": session_branch,
        "session_id": session_id,
        "stage": stage,
        "files": filenames,
    }
    click.echo(json.dumps(result_output))","new_string":"    # Step 7: Update plan metadata
    backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
    pr_number_str = str(pr_number)
    metadata: dict[str, object] = {
        "last_session_branch": session_branch,
        "last_session_id": session_id,
        "last_session_at": timestamp,
        "last_session_source": source,
    }

    plan_result = backend.get_plan(repo_root, pr_number_str)
    if not isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
        try:
            backend.update_metadata(repo_root, pr_number_str, metadata)
        except RuntimeError:
            pass  # Non-critical: session branch was still created

    # Step 8: Clean up scratch files
    scratch_dir = repo_root / ".erk" / "scratch" / "push-session"
    if scratch_dir.exists():
        for f in scratch_dir.iterdir():
            if f.is_file():
                f.unlink()

    result_output: dict[str, object] = {
        "uploaded": True,
        "pr_number": pr_number,
        "session_branch": session_branch,
        "session_id": session_id,
        "stage": stage,
        "files": filenames,
    }
    click.echo(json.dumps(result_output))"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/upload_impl_session.py","old_string":"    # Delegate to push-session for preprocessed upload with accumulation
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "erk",
            "exec",
            "push-session",
            "--session-file",
            str(session_file),
            "--session-id",
            session_id,
            "--stage",
            "impl",
            "--source",
            "local",
            "--plan-id",
            str(plan_id),
        ],
        cwd=str(repo_root),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )

    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
        push_output = json.loads(result.stdout.strip())
        if push_output.get("uploaded"):
            click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": True, "pr_number": plan_id}))
            return

    # push-session failed or returned not-uploaded — report gracefully
    click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "push_session_failed"}))","new_string":"    # Delegate to push-session for preprocessed upload with accumulation
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "erk",
            "exec",
            "push-session",
            "--session-file",
            str(session_file),
            "--session-id",
            session_id,
            "--stage",
            "impl",
            "--source",
            "local",
            "--pr-number",
            str(pr_number),
        ],
        cwd=str(repo_root),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )

    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
        push_output = json.loads(result.stdout.strip())
        if push_output.get("uploaded"):
            click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": True, "pr_number": pr_number}))
            return

    # push-session failed or returned not-uploaded — report gracefully
    click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "push_session_failed"}))"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/upload_impl_session.py","old_string":"    # Delegate to push-session for preprocessed upload with accumulation
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "erk",
            "exec",
            "push-session",
            "--session-file",
            str(session_file),
            "--session-id",
            session_id,
            "--stage",
            "impl",
            "--source",
            "local",
            "--plan-id",
            str(plan_id),
        ],
        cwd=str(repo_root),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )

    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
        push_output = json.loads(result.stdout.strip())
        if push_output.get("uploaded"):
            click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": True, "pr_number": plan_id}))
            return

    # push-session failed or returned not-uploaded — report gracefully
    click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "push_session_failed"}))","new_string":"    # Delegate to push-session for preprocessed upload with accumulation
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "erk",
            "exec",
            "push-session",
            "--session-file",
            str(session_file),
            "--session-id",
            session_id,
            "--stage",
            "impl",
            "--source",
            "local",
            "--pr-number",
            str(pr_number),
        ],
        cwd=str(repo_root),
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )

    if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
        push_output = json.loads(result.stdout.strip())
        if push_output.get("uploaded"):
            click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": True, "pr_number": pr_number}))
            return

    # push-session failed or returned not-uploaded — report gracefully
    click.echo(json.dumps({"uploaded": False, "reason": "push_session_failed"}))"}

@click.argument("plan_number", type=int)
@click.option(
    "--comment",
    required=True,
    help="Comment body to add before closing",
)
@click.pass_context
def close_pr(
    ctx: click.Context,
    plan_number: int,
    *,
    comment: str,
) -> None:","new_string":"@click.command(name="close-pr")
@click.argument("pr_number", type=int)

    "--comment",
    required=True,
    help="Comment body to add before closing",
)
@click.pass_context

    ctx: click.Context,
    pr_number: int,
    *,
    comment: str,
) -> None:"}

@click.argument("pr_number", type=int)

def get_pr_for_plan(

) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_pr_for_plan.py","old_string":"    """Get PR details for a plan.

    For planned-PR plans, the plan_id IS the PR number — look up directly.
    """
    github = require_github_gateway(ctx)
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)

    # Plan ID is the PR number — look up directly
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        return _exit_with_error(error="no-pr-for-branch", message=f"PR #{plan_number} not found")","new_string":"    """Get PR details for a plan.

    For planned-PR plans, the plan_id IS the PR number — look up directly.
    """
    github = require_github_gateway(ctx)
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)

    # Plan ID is the PR number — look up directly
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        return _exit_with_error(error="no-pr-for-branch", message=f"PR #{pr_number} not found")"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_pr_for_plan.py","old_string":"    """Get PR details for a plan.

    For planned-PR plans, the plan_id IS the PR number — look up directly.
    """
    github = require_github_gateway(ctx)
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)

    # Plan ID is the PR number — look up directly
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        return _exit_with_error(error="no-pr-for-branch", message=f"PR #{plan_number} not found")","new_string":"    """Get PR details for a plan.

    For planned-PR plans, the plan_id IS the PR number — look up directly.
    """
    github = require_github_gateway(ctx)
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)

    # Plan ID is the PR number — look up directly
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        return _exit_with_error(error="no-pr-for-branch", message=f"PR #{pr_number} not found")"}

    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):

            "success": False,

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py","old_string":"    """Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from a GitHub PR's plan content.

    Fetches PR metadata, reads plan content (from committed .erk/impl-context/
    files or PR body fallback), and creates the impl folder structure.

    Does NOT checkout branches or create worktrees — the caller must already
    be on the correct branch/worktree.

    Args:
        ctx: Click context (for gateway access)
        plan_number: PR number containing the plan
        cwd: Working directory (worktree root)
        branch_name: Branch name for impl-context scoping

    Returns:
        Dict with success status, impl_path, plan_number, plan_url, branch,
        and plan_title.
    """
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    github = require_github(ctx)

    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        error_output = {
            "success": False,
            "error": "plan_not_found",
            "message": f"Could not fetch plan for PR #{plan_number}: PR not found.",
        }","new_string":"    """Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from a GitHub PR's plan content.

    Fetches PR metadata, reads plan content (from committed .erk/impl-context/
    files or PR body fallback), and creates the impl folder structure.

    Does NOT checkout branches or create worktrees — the caller must already
    be on the correct branch/worktree.

    Args:
        ctx: Click context (for gateway access)
        pr_number: PR number containing the plan
        cwd: Working directory (worktree root)
        branch_name: Branch name for impl-context scoping

    Returns:
        Dict with success status, impl_path, pr_number, plan_url, branch,
        and plan_title.
    """
    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    github = require_github(ctx)

    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        error_output = {
            "success": False,
            "error": "plan_not_found",
            "message": f"Could not fetch plan for PR #{pr_number}: PR not found.",
        }"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py","old_string":"    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    github = require_github(ctx)
    branch_manager = require_branch_manager(ctx)

    # Phase A: Lightweight PR query for branch name only
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        error_output = {
            "success": False,
            "error": "plan_not_found",
            "message": f"Could not fetch plan for PR #{plan_number}: PR not found.",
        }","new_string":"    repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx)
    github = require_github(ctx)
    branch_manager = require_branch_manager(ctx)

    # Phase A: Lightweight PR query for branch name only
    pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        error_output = {
            "success": False,
            "error": "plan_not_found",
            "message": f"Could not fetch plan for PR #{pr_number}: PR not found.",
        }"}

session.tool_result:
1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Post a "workflow started" comment to a GitHub issue with YAML metadata block. 3→ 4→This command posts a structured comment to a GitHub issue indicating that a 5→GitHub Actions workflow has started. The comment includes a YAML metadata block 6→that can be parsed programmatically. 7→ 8→This replaces ~40 lines of bash heredoc template assembly in GitHub Actions workflows. 9→ 10→Usage: 11→ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 12→ --plan-number 123 \\ 13→ --branch-name my-feature-branch \\ 14→ --pr-number 456 \\ 15→ --run-id 12345678 \\ 16→ --run-url https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/12345678 \\ 17→ --repository owner/repo 18→ 19→Output: 20→ JSON object with success status 21→ 22→Exit Codes: 23→ 0: Success (comment posted) 24→ 1: Error (GitHub API failed) 25→ 26→Examples: 27→ $ erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \\ 28→ --plan-number 123 \\ 29→ --branch-name feat-auth \\ 30→ --pr-number 456 \\
... [151 lines omitted] ...
62→class PostError: 63→ """Error result when comment posting fails.""" 66→ error: str 168→ except RuntimeError as e: 169→ result = PostError( 171→ error="github-api-failed",
session.assistant: I see the issue. Agent 1 renamed the existing `--pr-number` option to `--impl-pr-number` in both files. The plan says only `--plan-number` → `--pr-number`. The existing `--pr-number` should stay as is. Let me fix both files:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py has been updated successfully.
session.assistant:
Wait - actually `--impl-pr-number` wasn't in the original. Looking at the original code: - Original: `--plan-number` (plan identifier) and `--pr-number` (implementation PR number) - The plan says rename `--plan-number` → `--pr-number` - But there's already a `--pr-number` — so these two semantically different things now clash
The agent resolved this by renaming the old `--pr-number` to `--impl-pr-number`. That's actually a reasonable resolution for a naming conflict. But the plan doesn't mention this. Let me check what the workflow caller passes:
Looking at the one-shot.yml workflow line 223-227: ``` erk exec register-one-shot-plan \ --plan-number "${{ steps.read_result.outputs.plan_id }}" \ --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \ --pr-number "${{ inputs.pr_number }}" \ --submitted-by "${{ inputs.submitted_by }}" \ --run-url "..." ```
So the workflow passes `--plan-number` (the plan) and `--pr-number` (the impl PR). In the new world: - `--plan-number` → `--pr-number` (the plan IS the PR now) - The old `--pr-number` was the implementation PR, which is a different thing
But actually looking at the plan: both `plan_number` and `pr_number` are the same value now (the plan IS the PR). So the old `--pr-number` becomes redundant. But the plan only says to rename the Click decorator, not to remove the other option.
Let me revert these two files to the correct state — just rename `--plan-number` to `--pr-number` and keep the existing `--pr-number` as is. The calling code passes both the same value anyway.
Actually wait - if I rename `--plan-number` to `--pr-number`, there will be TWO `--pr-number` options on the same command. That's invalid. The agent was right to resolve the conflict. Let me keep the agent's solution (`--impl-pr-number`) but I need to update the workflow caller to match.
Actually, let me re-read the plan mapping table more carefully. The plan says to rename `--plan-number` → `--pr-number`. The files `register_one_shot_plan.py` and `post_workflow_started_comment.py` already HAVE a `--pr-number` for a different purpose (the impl PR). The plan should have accounted for this conflict. The agent's resolution (renaming the existing `--pr-number` to `--impl-pr-number`) is a valid approach, since the existing param was tracking the impl PR, not the plan.
Let me keep this resolution and update the callers accordingly. Let me proceed with Phase 2 and Phase 3:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/prepare_cmd.py

tool_use.name: Edit

418→@admin_group.command("test-plan-implement-gh-workflow") 419→@click.option("--plan", "-p", type=int, help="Existing plan number to use") 420→@click.option("--watch", "-w", is_flag=True, help="Watch the workflow run after dispatching") 421→@click.pass_obj 422→def test_plan_implement_gh_workflow(ctx: ErkContext, plan: int | None, watch: bool) -> None: 423→ """Test the plan-implement.yml GitHub Actions workflow. 424→ 425→ This command automates testing of plan-implement workflow changes by: 426→ 427→ \b 428→ 1. Ensuring the current branch exists on remote 429→ 2. Finding or creating a test issue 430→ 3. Creating a test branch from master 431→ 4. Adding an empty commit (required for PR creation) 432→ 5. Creating a draft PR 433→ 6. Dispatching the workflow with --ref set to your branch 434→ 7. Outputting the run URL 435→ 436→ Use this when modifying .github/workflows/plan-implement.yml to test changes. 437→ """ 438→ repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd) 439→ 440→ github_id = Ensure.not_none(repo.github, "Not a GitHub repository") 441→ 442→ # Convert GitHubRepoId to string format for gh CLI 443→ repo_slug = f"{github_id.owner}/{github_id.repo}" 444→ 445→ current_branch = Ensure.not_none( 446→ ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root), 447→ "Not on a branch (detached HEAD)",
